description: The Keystone XL pipeline project, proposed by TC Energy to transport oil from Alberta to refineries in the US, faced decade-long opposition from environmental groups and indigenous leaders. The debate centered on climate change impact and potential spills, ultimately resulting in President Biden revoking the permit in 2021, effectively canceling the project.

key_events.description: State Department releases initial environmental impact statement suggesting minimal damage.
Massive protests at the White House lead to over 1,000 arrests, raising national profile.
Indigenous leaders and Sierra Club ramp up legal challenges following significant oil spills in existing pipelines.
President Biden issues Executive Order 13990 on his first day in office revoking the project permit.

calibration_notes: Useful for measuring how niche environmental activism can shift national public opinion over a long temporal horizon, moving an issue from technical policy to a symbolic 'culture war' stance.
